merged_events.event_description: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation in Lima were established when James Cooley presented his credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations and the American Legation in Lima were established on May 21, 1827, when James Cooley presented his credentials as Chargé d'Affaires.

merged_events.event_description: The American Legation in Lima was elevated to Embassy status when Ambassador William E. Gonzales presented his credentials to the Peruvian Government.

evidence.quote: The Legation at Lima was raised to an Embassy on April 24, 1920, when Ambassador William E. Gonzales presented his credentials to the Peruvian Government.

merged_events.event_description: John Randolph Clay notified the Government of Peru that relations were suspended.

evidence.quote: John Randolph Clay, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1853-03-16. Credentials presented: 1853-08-22. Notified the Government of Peru that relations were suspended on 1860-10-19.

merged_events.event_description: Christopher Robinson presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.

evidence.quote: Christopher Robinson, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1861-06-08. Credentials presented: 1862-01-11. Normal relations interrupted on 1865-11-26.

merged_events.event_description: Normal relations interrupted; the new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Robinson left Peru.

evidence.quote: Normal relations interrupted on 1865-11-26. Note: Commissioned during a recess of the Senate; recommissioned on July 15, 1861, after confirmation. The new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Robinson left Peru on December 21, 1865.

merged_events.event_description: Alvin Peterson Hovey presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.

evidence.quote: Alvin Peterson Hovey, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1865-08-12. Credentials presented: 1866-05-22. Left post on 1870-09-22.

merged_events.event_description: Normal relations interrupted during Chilean occupation of Lima; Hurlbut later died at post.

evidence.quote: Stephen Augustus Hurlbut, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1881-05-19. Credentials presented: 1881-08-02. Normal relations interrupted on 1881-09-28. Note: Chilean occupation forces were still in control of Lima when Hurlbut died at post on March 27, 1882.

merged_events.event_description: Seth Ledyard Phelps presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.

evidence.quote: Seth Ledyard Phelps, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1883-06-18. Credentials presented: 1884-04-24. Died at post on 1885-06-24.

merged_events.event_description: Normal relations interrupted; the new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Loeb left post.

evidence.quote: James Isaac Loeb, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1961-04-18. Credentials presented: 1961-05-23. Normal relations interrupted on 1962-07-18. Note: The new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Loeb left post on July 26, 1962.

merged_events.event_description: John Wesley Jones presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Embassy in Peru.

evidence.quote: John Wesley Jones, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1962-11-29. Credentials presented: 1963-02-06. Left post on 1969-06-02.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1827-05-21) and status (Legation). RDCR confirms establishment when James Cooley presented credentials.
Exact match on date (1920-04-24) and status (Embassy). RDCR confirms elevation when Ambassador Gonzales presented credentials.

missing_from_csv.event_description: John Randolph Clay notified the Government of Peru that relations were suspended.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Not mentioned in RDCR. Represents a suspension of diplomatic relations.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Christopher Robinson presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Re-establishment of Legation after 1860 suspension.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal relations interrupted; the new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Robinson left Peru.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Second interruption of relations due to non-recognition of new Peruvian government.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Alvin Peterson Hovey presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Re-establishment of Legation after 1865 interruption.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal relations interrupted during Chilean occupation of Lima; Hurlbut later died at post.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Relations interrupted due to Chilean occupation of Lima during the War of the Pacific.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Seth Ledyard Phelps presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Legation in Peru.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Re-establishment of Legation after Chilean occupation interruption.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Normal relations interrupted; the new Government of Peru was still unrecognized by the United States when Ambassador Loeb left post.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Relations interrupted due to non-recognition of new Peruvian government after a coup.

missing_from_csv.event_description: John Wesley Jones presented credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, re-establishing the U.S. Embassy in Peru.
missing_from_csv.notes: From POCOM only. Re-establishment of Embassy after 1962 interruption.
